shapes.title: 🎨 Shapes in Art
shapes.html:
Look around you! Can you see shapes? Shapes are everywhere — in buildings, in nature and in art.

A triangle has three sides. Many roofs are triangles. A diamond looks like a square turned sideways. You can see diamonds on playing cards!

An oval is like a long circle — eggs are ovals! A spiral goes round and round, getting bigger and bigger. Snail shells are beautiful spirals.

Artists use shapes to make amazing art. Some artists compare the size of shapes — big circles next to small circles look very interesting.

You can find shapes in nature too. Flowers have oval petals. Spider webs have spiral patterns. Leaves have straight lines running through them. Snowflakes have triangle shapes inside!

Look at a photograph of a city. Can you see triangles on the roofs? Can you see ovals in the windows? Shapes are all around us — you just need to look!

artclass.title: ✂️ Art Class
artclass.html:
Today is art class! The teacher says: 'We are going to make art with shapes.'

'Are there any triangles on the table?' asks Mia. 'Yes, there are lots of triangles!' says the teacher. 'Are there any circles?' asks Tom. 'Yes! There are big circles and small circles.'

'Are there any diamonds?' asks Lily. 'There aren't any diamonds yet — you can cut some!' says the teacher.

First, the children cut out shapes from coloured paper. Tom makes a pattern with triangles — big, small, big, small. Mia makes a beautiful spiral with strips of paper.

Lily uses origami to fold a paper bird. 'There isn't any glue on my table!' says Lily. 'That is OK,' says the teacher. 'Origami doesn't need glue — only folding!'

At the end, the children put their art on the wall. Tom makes a drawing of all the shapes. The teacher takes a photograph of the wall. She hangs the art as a mobile from the ceiling. It moves in the wind!

'Are there any more shapes to find?' asks Tom. 'Always!' says the teacher. 'Shapes are everywhere!'
